steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is an Inferences question. The passage establishes a three-link chain: rewards trigger dopamine production; dopamine is required for fan cell activity; fan cells are necessary for forming associative memories. The word "Consequently" signals we must follow that chain to its logical conclusion.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — decrease an individual's capacity to utilize dopamine.: the chain runs in the opposite direction — rewards increase dopamine, which activates fan cells. Nothing in the passage suggests rewards reduce dopamine capacity. ✗
  • Choice B — increase an individual's capacity to recognize differences between unrelated things.: associative memory is defined as remembering connections between unrelated things, not recognizing differences between them. This statement distorts the definition. ✗
  • Choice C — increase an individual's capacity to form associative memories.: rewards produce dopamine; dopamine activates fan cells; fan cells enable associative memory formation. Following the chain, rewards should increase the capacity to form associative memories. ✓
  • Choice D — decrease an individual's capacity to create fan cells.: the passage says dopamine is required for fan cell activity, not that rewards control how many fan cells are created. The text does not discuss fan cell creation at all. ✗
C is correct.
